page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and features a 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across memory and compute-intensive tasks. The instance demonstrates high memory bandwidth, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small-block reads at 4110.43 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 222.41 GB/sec. CPU-bound workloads benefit from advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning. This is reflected in strong Geekbench multi-core scores of 18732.0 and Passmark CPU extended instructions tests. Database and web serving capabilities are also notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 64.03 million operations per second and static web serving throughput recorded at 73.67 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex family offers a resource-dense profile utilizing DDR5 memory and Gen7 Intel Xeon processors without the overhead of bundled local storage. This makes it a viable option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage. Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and text processing, the m7i-flex.12xlarge is well-suited for enterprise applications, web serving, database hosting, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.2 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage but delivers a baseline network speed of 9 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression. The hardware configuration excels in cryptographic and vector calculations, as demonstrated by high scores in Passmark extended instructions and encryption tests. This resource-dense, general-purpose profile is highly efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory without local storage overhead, making it suitable for web serving, database hosting, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor, optimized for balanced compute and memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and Intel AMX technology for scalable enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Delivers 9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for web serving, database hosting, and software compilation
